demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis record against facility face sheet revealed a zip code mismatch (19082 vs 19083). Insurance verified as Straight Medicare with active Part B coverage.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirms the goal is Home with 24/7 family support; patient is not a candidate for LTC at this time.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, first time hearing of the program. Q2: Thinks we are hospital transport. Q3: Yes, SW explained RN/PT visits. Q4: Very anxious about oxygen tank management at home; PN escalated anxiety to SW for additional RT education before discharge.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient stable on 2L NC; PT reports patient is modified independent with rolling walker. SW targeting Friday discharge.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into daughter's phone; program flyer delivered to patient's bedside folder.
hha_confirmation_status: Not In Place. Barrier: Provider Illness. The HHA's designated respiratory-trained nurse is out sick; no alternate nurse available in the zip code until Monday. PN escalated to SW to delay D/C until HHA is 'In Place'.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed D/C date shifted to Monday to ensure HHA coverage. Call 2 (to family): Confirmed they are aware of the new Monday start-of-care window and that oxygen has been delivered to the home.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to Monday discharge; home readiness confirmed, and family understands the HHA nurse will arrive between 9 AM and 11 AM.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date of 10/23 and first HHA visit date of 10/23 into Atlantis queue.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Monday 10/23 at 2:00 PM, following HHA 'In Place' confirmation for the morning window.
narrative_summary: Managed the transition of an 83yo post-ICU pneumonia patient by first identifying respiratory anxieties during the Connection Intake and escalating to the SW. During the Maintenance stage, a provider illness at the HHA created a 3-day coverage gap, which the PN addressed by coordinating a D/C delay with the SW to maintain the Confidence Loop. The transition was secured in Stage 3 after the HHA confirmed 'In Place' status for Monday, allowing for the successful scheduling of the MA visit within the 24-hour window.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis demographic audit reveals zip code error; requested SW update to ensure HHA referral accuracy.
HHA intake coordinator reports the assigned respiratory nurse is out with flu; no backup available for Friday.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient expresses high anxiety regarding home oxygen use during Stage 1 meeting.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated Q4 intake response to SW to request a respiratory therapy consult prior to discharge.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA is unable to confirm a nurse for the Friday discharge due to staff illness.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Advised SW that HHA is 'Not In Place' and recommended holding MA scheduling until Monday coverage is verified.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter is confused about the difference between the HHA nurse and the MA.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted education on the Day 1 plan, explaining the HHA focuses on clinical monitoring while the MA supports the transition loop.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to find a different HHA that has a nurse available for a Friday discharge to avoid delaying the patient's exit.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role in agency selection and avoids interference, though it may result in a less specialized nurse for the patient's respiratory needs.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Perform the Confidence Audit by calling the HHA intake coordinator to confirm when the specialist nurse returns, then relaying this 'Not In Place' status to the SW to align the discharge date with safe home coverage.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the HHA-First Rule, ensuring the MA is not scheduled for a failed visit and that the patient has the required skilled specialist in place before leaving the facility.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call three alternative HHA agencies in the area to see if they have respiratory nurses available and then present those options to the family to speed up the Friday discharge.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: While this seems like helpful advocacy, it violates the SW Boundary by performing agency outreach and selection, which is strictly the Social Worker's responsibility.
